"""

from manim import *


class AutoScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Title
        title = Text("Dead ReLU Neurons", font_size=42, color=YELLOW)
        title.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(title), run_time=0.8)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Create axes for ReLU function

        self.play(Create(axes), Write(x_label), Write(y_label), run_time=1)

        relu_graph = axes.plot(

            width=axes.c2p(0, 0)[0] - axes.c2p(-4, 0)[0],

            fill_opacity=0.3,
            stroke_color=RED,
            stroke_width=2,

            0
        ])
        
        dead_text = Text("DEAD ZONE", color=RED, font_size=18)

        self.play(FadeIn(dead_zone), Write(dead_text), run_time=0.8)

        self.play(FadeIn(sample_point), Write(output_text), run_time=0.6)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Gradient explanation

            stroke_width=5,

        self.play(
            FadeOut(dead_text),
            Write(grad_text),
            GrowArrow(zero_arrow),
            run_time=0.8
        )
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Weight update explanation
        self.play(
            FadeOut(zero_arrow),
            FadeOut(grad_text),
            FadeOut(output_text),
            FadeOut(sample_point),
            run_time=0.3
        )
        
        # Weight update equations
        weight_eq = MathTex(
            "w_{\\text{new}} = w_{\\text{old}} - \\eta \\cdot \\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial w}",
            color=WHITE

"""
Dead ReLU Neurons Visualization
Shows how negative inputs kill gradients and prevent weight updates.
"""

from manim import *


class AutoScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Title
        title = Text("Dead ReLU Neurons", font_size=48, color=YELLOW)
        subtitle = Text("The Vanishing Gradient Problem", font_size=24, color=GRAY)
        subtitle.next_to(title, DOWN)
        
        title_group = VGroup(title, subtitle)
        title_group.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        
        self.play(Write(title), run_time=1)
        self.play(FadeIn(subtitle), run_time=0.5)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Create axes for ReLU function
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            y_range=[-1, 4, 1],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True, "numbers_to_exclude": [0]},
        )
        axes.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1)
        
        # Labels
        x_label = MathTex("x").next_to(axes.x_axis.get_end(), RIGHT)
        y_label = MathTex("f(x)").next_to(axes.y_axis.get_end(), UP)
        
        self.play(Create(axes), Write(x_label), Write(y_label), run_time=1.5)
        
        # ReLU function: f(x) = max(0, x)
        relu_graph = axes.plot(
            lambda x: max(0, x),
            x_range=[-4, 4],
            color=BLUE,
            stroke_width=4,
        )
        
        relu_label = MathTex("f(x) = \\max(0, x)", color=BLUE).scale(0.8)
        relu_label.to_corner(UR, buff=1)
        
        self.play(
            Create(relu_graph),
            Write(relu_label),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show negative region (dead zone)
        dead_zone = Rectangle(
            width=axes.c2p(0, 0)[0] - axes.c2p(-4, 0)[0],
            height=axes.c2p(0, 4)[1] - axes.c2p(0, -1)[1],
            fill_color=RED,
            fill_opacity=0.2,
            stroke_color=RED,
            stroke_opacity=0.5,
        )
        dead_zone.move_to(
            [
                (axes.c2p(-4, 0)[0] + axes.c2p(0, 0)[0]) / 2,
                (axes.c2p(0, -1)[1] + axes.c2p(0, 4)[1]) / 2,
                0
            ]
        )
        
        dead_text = Text("DEAD ZONE", color=RED, font_size=20)
        dead_text.move_to(dead_zone.get_center())
        
        self.play(
            FadeIn(dead_zone),
            Write(dead_text),
            run_time=1
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show a sample input point in the dead zone
        sample_x = -2
        sample_point = Dot(axes.c2p(sample_x, 0), color=RED, radius=0.1)
        sample_label = MathTex(f"x = {sample_x}", color=RED).scale(0.7)
        sample_label.next_to(sample_point, DOWN)
        
        output_text = MathTex(f"f({sample_x}) = 0", color=WHITE).scale(0.8)
        output_text.next_to(axes, LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        self.play(
            FadeIn(sample_point),
            Write(sample_label),
            run_time=0.8
        )
        self.play(Write(output_text), run_time=0.7)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show gradient (derivative)
        self.play(
            FadeOut(dead_text),
            FadeOut(sample_label),
        )
        
        # Gradient annotation
        gradient_box = VGroup()
        grad_title = Text("Gradient (Derivative):", font_size=20, color=YELLOW)
        grad_eq = MathTex("\\frac{df}{dx} = 0 \\text{ for } x < 0", color=RED).scale(0.8)
        grad_eq2 = MathTex("\\frac{df}{dx} = 1 \\text{ for } x > 0", color=GREEN).scale(0.8)
        
        grad_title.to_corner(UL, buff=0.5)
        grad_eq.next_to(grad_title, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        grad_eq2.next_to(grad_eq, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        
        gradient_box.add(grad_title, grad_eq, grad_eq2)
        
        self.play(
            Write(grad_title),
            run_time=0.5
        )
        self.play(
            Write(grad_eq),
            run_time=0.7
        )
        self.play(
            Write(grad_eq2),
            run_time=0.7
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show zero gradient arrow
        zero_grad = Arrow(
            sample_point.get_center() + UP * 0.3,
            sample_point.get_center() + DOWN * 0.3,
            color=RED,
            buff=0,
            stroke_width=6,
        )
        zero_grad_label = Text("Zero Gradient!", color=RED, font_size=18)
        zero_grad_label.next_to(zero_grad, LEFT)
        
        self.play(
            GrowArrow(zero_grad),
            Write(zero_grad_label),
            run_time=0.8
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show the consequence: no weight update
        self.play(
            FadeOut(zero_grad),
            FadeOut(zero_grad_label),
            FadeOut(gradient_box),
            run_time=0.5
        )
        
        # Weight update equation
        self.play(
            FadeOut(relu_label),
            run_time=0.3
        )
        
        weight_title = Text("Weight Update (Backprop):", font_size=22, color=YELLOW)
        weight_title.to_corner(UR, buff=0.5)
        
        weight_eq = MathTex(
            "w_{\\text{new}} = w_{\\text{old}} - \\eta \\cdot \\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial w}",
            color=WHITE
        ).scale(0.75)
        weight_eq.next_to(weight_title, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        
        chain_eq = MathTex(
            "\\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial w} = \\underbrace{\\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial f}}_{\\text{upstream}} \\cdot \\underbrace{\\frac{df}{dx}}_{\\mathbf{0}}",
            color=RED
        ).scale(0.7)
        chain_eq.next_to(weight_eq, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        result_eq = MathTex("\\Rightarrow w_{\\text{new}} = w_{\\text{old}}", color=YELLOW).scale(0.8)
        result_eq.next_to(chain_eq, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        # Cross out the weight update to emphasize nothing changes
        cross_line = Line(
            weight_eq.get_left(),
            weight_eq.get_right(),
            color=RED,
            stroke_width=3
        )
        
        self.play(Write(weight_title), run_time=0.5)
        self.play(Write(weight_eq), run_time=1)
        self.wait(0.3)
        self.play(Write(chain_eq), run_time=1.2)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Highlight that gradient is zero
        zero_box = SurroundingRectangle(
            chain_eq[0][-3:-1],  # Try to highlight the 0 part
            color=RED,
            buff=0.1
        )
        self.play(Create(zero_box), run_time=0.5)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        self.play(Write(result_eq), run_time=0.8)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Final dead neuron indicator
        skull = Text("☠", font_size=72, color=RED)
        skull.move_to(dead_zone.get_center())
        
        self.play(
            FadeOut(sample_point),
            FadeOut(output_text),
            Transform(dead_zone, skull),
            run_time=1
        )
        
        dead_label = Text("DEAD NEURON", font_size=28, color=RED, weight=BOLD)
        dead_label.next_to(skull, DOWN)
        
        final_text = Text("Weights frozen forever!", font_size=20, color=GRAY)
        final_text.next_to(dead_label, DOWN)
        
        self.play(
            Write(dead_label),
            run_time=0.7
        )
        self.play(
            Write(final_text),
            run_time=0.6
        )
        
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Fade everything out
        self.play(
            FadeOut(title),
            FadeOut(subtitle),
            FadeOut(axes),
            FadeOut(x_label),
            FadeOut(y_label),
            FadeOut(relu_graph),
            FadeOut(dead_zone),
            FadeOut(skull),
            FadeOut(dead_label),
            FadeOut(final_text),
            FadeOut(weight_title),
            FadeOut(weight_eq),
            FadeOut(chain_eq),
            FadeOut(result_eq),
            FadeOut(zero_box),
            run_time=1
        )
        
        # Final summary
        summary = Text(
            "ReLU dies when x < 0 → gradient = 0 → weights never update",
            font_size=28,
            color=YELLOW
        )
        self.play(Write(summary), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)
        self.play(FadeOut(summary), run_time=0.5)

content.text: Welcome to the Dead ReLU Neuron visualization. Today, we'll explore one of the fundamental problems in deep learning. The ReLU activation function outputs zero for all negative inputs, and the input value for all positive inputs. This red shaded region is the dead zone. When a neuron's input falls here, the output is always zero. Look at this example. When x equals negative two, the output is zero. The gradient, or derivative, of ReLU is zero for all negative inputs, and one for all positive inputs. Zero gradient means no signal flows backward through the network. During backpropagation, weights are updated using the gradient. The chain rule multiplies gradients through each layer. But when the ReLU gradient is zero, the entire product becomes zero. This means the weight update term vanishes. The result? The new weight equals the old weight. Nothing changes. The neuron is now dead. Its weights are frozen forever, no matter how long you train. Remember: ReLU dies when x is less than zero, which means gradient equals zero, which means weights never update.

# Text-to-speech (TTS) in EduClaw

EduClaw includes a **pluggable TTS subsystem** under `educlaw.tts`: a small `TTSBackend` protocol, a registry (built-ins + `importlib.metadata` entry points, same pattern as [`educlaw.channels`](../src/educlaw/channels/registry.py)), and optional **Kitten TTS** integration for **offline-friendly**, CPU-only synthesis with compact ONNX models (~25–80 MB on disk).

**See also:** [EduClaw_Concepts_Explained.md](EduClaw_Concepts_Explained.md) (subsystem 13), [DEVELOPERS.md](DEVELOPERS.md) (gateway `/ws` chat vs CLI), [AUTOCOURSE.md](AUTOCOURSE.md) (full-course scripts; generate lecture text then pass it to `educlaw tts say` or the TTS API if you need audio).

## Why it exists

- **Offline / air-gapped labs**: cache models once, then set `HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` for Hugging Face Hub.
- **Small footprint**: Kitten ships int8 and small-parameter checkpoints suitable for laptops and edge boxes.
- **Modularity**: swap backends via profile or register your own via entry points.

## Quick enable (Kitten, CPU)

1. **Install the wheel and extras** (Kitten publishes wheels on GitHub Releases, not only PyPI):

   ```bash
   pip install 'educlaw[tts-kitten]'
   ```

   The `tts-kitten` extra pins the official wheel URL from [KittenTTS releases](https://github.com/KittenML/KittenTTS/releases).

2. **Pre-download a model** (first run may hit the Hub; set `HF_HOME` / cache dir as you prefer):

   ```bash
   python -c "from kittentts import KittenTTS; KittenTTS('KittenML/kitten-tts-nano-0.8-int8', cache_dir=str(__import__('pathlib').Path.home()/'.educlaw/tts'))"
   ```

3. **Profile** (`profiles/local.toml` under `[educlaw]`):

   ```toml
   tts_enabled = true
   tts_backend = "kitten"
   tts_model_id = "KittenML/kitten-tts-nano-0.8-int8"
   tts_voice = "Jasper"
   tts_speed = 1.0
   ```

   **`tts_model_id` is required** when `tts_backend = "kitten"` — there is no baked-in default model id.

4. **Air-gapped use**: after the model is cached under `tts_cache_dir` (default `data_dir/tts`), export:

   ```bash
   export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1
   ```

## WebSocket protocol (planned)

The following JSON frame shape is **not** implemented on the gateway **`/ws`** endpoint today; use **`educlaw tts say`** or call `build_backend(settings)` from Python. A future muxed WebSocket could adopt:

After a `connect` frame, clients would send:

```json
{"type": "tts", "text": "Hello from EduClaw.", "voice": "Jasper", "speed": 1.0, "idempotency_key": "optional"}
```

Responses (in order):

- `{"type":"tts_event","payload":{"kind":"audio","format":"wav","sample_rate":24000,"voice":"Jasper","bytes_b64":"..."}}`
- `{"type":"tts_event","payload":{"kind":"done"}}`

Errors would use `payload.kind` of `error` and `payload.message`. If `tts_enabled` is false, synthesis is unavailable at the API layer. Shield / classify behavior matches other EduClaw entry points when wired.

## CLI

| Command | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `educlaw tts list` | List registered backend names; if `tts_enabled`, build the active backend and print `available_voices`. |
| `educlaw tts say "Your text" -o out.wav` | Synthesize to a WAV file (requires `tts_enabled` and a working backend). |

## Tuning knobs (`Settings` / env `EDUCLAW_*`)

| Field | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `tts_enabled` | Master switch; when false, `build_backend` returns no synthesizer for scripts/API use. |
| `tts_backend` | Registry key: `kitten`, `null` (silent stub for tests), or a custom entry-point name. |
| `tts_model_id` | **Required for `kitten`**: Hugging Face repo id, e.g. `KittenML/kitten-tts-nano-0.8-int8`. |
| `tts_voice` | Default voice when using the API programmatically. |
| `tts_speed` | Default speed multiplier. |
| `tts_sample_rate` | Hint for the `null` backend; Kitten emits 24 kHz PCM internally. |
| `tts_cache_dir` | Model cache directory (default `data_dir/tts`). |

### Kitten model sizes (reference)

| Model | Params | Approx. size |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| kitten-tts-nano-0.8-int8 | 15M | ~25 MB |
| kitten-tts-nano-0.8 | 15M | ~56 MB |
| kitten-tts-micro-0.8 | 40M | ~41 MB |
| kitten-tts-mini-0.8 | 80M | ~80 MB |

See the upstream [KittenTTS README](https://github.com/KittenML/KittenTTS) for updates.

## Adding a custom backend

1. Implement `TTSBackend` from `educlaw.tts.contract` (`name`, `available_voices`, async `synthesize`, async `close`).
2. Expose a factory `def factory(settings: Settings) -> TTSBackend: ...`
3. Register either:
   - **In-process**: `from educlaw.tts.registry import register` then `register("mytts", factory)` before `build_backend` runs, or
   - **Packaged**: in your `pyproject.toml`:

     ```toml
     [project.entry-points."educlaw.tts"]
     mytts = "my_package.tts:factory"
     ```

Entry-point names override built-ins with the same key.

## Troubleshooting

- **`ImportError: kittentts`**: install `educlaw[tts-kitten]` (includes the GitHub wheel).
- **`RuntimeError: Unknown TTS backend`**: typo in `tts_backend` — run `educlaw tts list` for keys.
- **`tts_model_id` unset** with `kitten`: set a Hub repo id in the profile.
- **Voice not found**: use a name from `available_voices` (Kitten ships Bella, Jasper, Luna, Bruno, Rosie, Hugo, Kiki, Leo by default).
- **Hub timeouts offline**: ensure the model is cached and `HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` only after a successful download.

"""
Dead ReLU Neurons Visualization
Shows how negative inputs kill gradients and prevent weight updates.
"""

from manim import *


class AutoScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Title
        title = Text("Dead ReLU Neurons", font_size=42, color=YELLOW)
        title.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(title), run_time=0.8)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Create axes for ReLU function
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            y_range=[-1, 4, 1],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=3.5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True},
        )
        axes.next_to(title, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        x_label = MathTex("x").next_to(axes.x_axis.get_end(), RIGHT)
        y_label = MathTex("f(x)").next_to(axes.y_axis.get_end(), UP)
        
        self.play(Create(axes), Write(x_label), Write(y_label), run_time=1)
        
        # ReLU function
        relu_graph = axes.plot(
            lambda x: max(0, x),
            x_range=[-4, 4],
            color=BLUE,
            stroke_width=4,
        )
        self.play(Create(relu_graph), run_time=1)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Dead zone
        dead_zone = Rectangle(
            width=axes.c2p(0, 0)[0] - axes.c2p(-4, 0)[0],
            height=axes.c2p(0, 4)[1] - axes.c2p(0, -1)[1],
            fill_color=RED,
            fill_opacity=0.3,
            stroke_color=RED,
            stroke_width=2,
        )
        dead_zone.move_to([
            (axes.c2p(-4, 0)[0] + axes.c2p(0, 0)[0]) / 2,
            (axes.c2p(0, -1)[1] + axes.c2p(0, 4)[1]) / 2,
            0
        ])
        
        dead_text = Text("DEAD ZONE", color=RED, font_size=18)
        dead_text.move_to(dead_zone.get_center())
        
        self.play(FadeIn(dead_zone), Write(dead_text), run_time=0.8)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Sample point
        sample_point = Dot(axes.c2p(-2, 0), color=RED, radius=0.1)
        output_text = MathTex("f(-2) = 0", color=WHITE).scale(0.7)
        output_text.next_to(axes, LEFT, buff=0.3)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(sample_point), Write(output_text), run_time=0.6)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Gradient explanation
        grad_text = MathTex("\\frac{df}{dx} = 0 \\text{ for } x < 0", color=RED).scale(0.7)
        grad_text.to_corner(UL, buff=0.3)
        
        zero_arrow = Arrow(
            sample_point.get_center() + UP * 0.2,
            sample_point.get_center() + DOWN * 0.2,
            color=RED,
            buff=0,
            stroke_width=5,
        )
        
        self.play(
            FadeOut(dead_text),
            Write(grad_text),
            GrowArrow(zero_arrow),
            run_time=0.8
        )
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Weight update explanation
        self.play(
            FadeOut(zero_arrow),
            FadeOut(grad_text),
            FadeOut(output_text),
            FadeOut(sample_point),
            run_time=0.3
        )
        
        # Weight update equations
        weight_eq = MathTex(
            "w_{\\text{new}} = w_{\\text{old}} - \\eta \\cdot \\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial w}",
            color=WHITE
        ).scale(0.65)
        weight_eq.next_to(axes, RIGHT, buff=0.3)
        
        chain_eq = MathTex(
            "\\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial w} = \\frac{\\partial L}{\\partial f} \\cdot \\mathbf{0} = 0",
            color=RED
        ).scale(0.6)
        chain_eq.next_to(weight_eq, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.3)
        
        result_eq = MathTex("\\Rightarrow w_{\\text{new}} = w_{\\text{old}}", color=YELLOW).scale(0.65)
        result_eq.next_to(chain_eq, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.3)
        
        self.play(Write(weight_eq), run_time=0.8)
        self.play(Write(chain_eq), run_time=0.8)
        self.play(Write(result_eq), run_time=0.6)
        self.wait(0.3)
        
        # Final dead neuron
        skull = Text("X", font_size=48, color=RED)
        skull.move_to(dead_zone.get_center())
        
        self.play(
            FadeOut(dead_zone, run_time=0.3),
            FadeIn(skull),
            run_time=0.6
        )
        
        dead_label = Text("DEAD NEURON", font_size=22, color=RED, weight=BOLD)
        dead_label.next_to(skull, DOWN)
        
        frozen_text = Text("Weights frozen!", font_size=16, color=GRAY)
        frozen_text.next_to(dead_label, DOWN)
        
        self.play(Write(dead_label), run_time=0.5)
        self.play(Write(frozen_text), run_time=0.4)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Summary
        self.play(
            FadeOut(title),
            FadeOut(axes),
            FadeOut(x_label),
            FadeOut(y_label),
            FadeOut(relu_graph),
            FadeOut(weight_eq),
            FadeOut(chain_eq),
            FadeOut(result_eq),
            FadeOut(skull),
            FadeOut(dead_label),
            FadeOut(frozen_text),
            run_time=0.8
        )
        
        summary = Text(
            "x < 0  →  gradient = 0  →  no weight update",
            font_size=26,
            color=YELLOW
        )
        self.play(Write(summary), run_time=1)
        self.wait(0.8)
        self.play(FadeOut(summary), run_time=0.5)
